english: or he) teaches how to avert, prevent etc. -- 2. v. sgrig-pa (sgrig pa). II. sbst. 1. knowing, knowledge; prudence, talents, natural gifts Glr.; rig-pa daṅ ldán-pa (rig pa dang ldan pa) talented, rich in knowledge, learned Dzl.; rig-pa gsar-ba (rig pa gsar ba) new informations, disclosures, knowledge; news, lóg-gi rig-pa bsgrés-na (log gi rig pa bsgres na) if one compares the absurd news Tar. 174, Schf.; ma-rig-pa (ma rig pa) 1. sbst. अविद्या ignorance, mostly used in the specific Buddhist sense, viz. for the innate principal and fundamental error of considering perishable things as permanent and of looking upon the external world as one really existing, with Buddhists in a certain manner the original sin, from which every evil is proceeding, v. Köpp. I, 163 (but cf. gti-mug (gti mug)). 2. adj. void of reason, unreasonable, irrational, dúd-gro ma-rig-pa (dud gro ma rig pa) Mil. -- 2. science, learning, literature, naṅ-gi rig-pa (nang gi rig pa) the orthodox or sacred literature, pyii rig-pa (phyi'i rig pa) the heterodox or profane literature Cs., tun-moṅ-gi rig-pa (thun mong gi rig pa) literature or science common to both religions (Buddhists and Brahmans) Cs.; rig-pai gnas (rig pa'i gnas) and rig-pa (rig pa) any single science (philosophy, medicine etc.) v. rig-gnás (rig gnas); rig-pai ról-tso (rig pa'i rol mtsho) or rig-pai gnas tams-cád (rig pa'i gnas thams cad) Cs. circle of science, encyclopedia. -- 3. soul (prob. only in later literature), rig-pa lus daṅ brál-ba (rig pa lus dang bral ba) the soul separated from the body, rten daṅ brál-ba (rten dang bral ba) the soul separated from her hold or from her abode Thgr.; often opp. to bem (bem) Mil. Comp. rig-mkán (rig mkhan), rig(-pa)-po (rig pa po) Cs., Sch. a knowing person, a learned man. -- rig-rgyud (rig rgyud) character Mil. -- rig-sṅags (rig sngags) a spell, charm, magic formula, rig-sṅags-mkán (rig sngags mkhan) a person skilled in charms. -- rig-gnás (rig gnas) a science, one of the sciences; rig-gnás ċé-ba lṅa (rig gnas che ba lnga) the five great sciences or classes of science, frq.; these are: sgrá-rig-pa (sgra rig pa) science of language, gtan-tsigs-rig-pa (gtan tshigs rig pa) dialectics, gsó-ba-rig-pa (gso ba rig pa) medicine, bzó-rig-pa (bzo rig pa) science of mechanical arts, naṅ-dón-rig-pa (nang don rig pa) religious philosophy; of less consequence are: rig-gnás ċuṅ-ba lṅa (rig gnas chung ba lnga) the five minor sciences; and the rig-gnás (rig gnas) or rig-pabċo-brgyad (rig pa bco brgyad) (also; tsug-lag (gtsug lag)?), which need not be particularly enumerated, though they are often mentioned in the Dzl.; they are named by Cs. and Sch. -- rig(-pa)-po (rig pa po) v. rig-mkán (rig mkhan). -- rig-byéd (rig byed) 1. conveying knowledge, instructive, prob. also learned, ṅa rig-byéd glú-mkán ma yín-te (nga rig byed glu mkhan ma yin te) I am no schooled, accomplished, singer Mil. 2. instruction, a book conveying knowledge, a scientific work, bzoi rig-byéd (bzo'i rig byed) a technological work Glr. 3. वेद Veda, the (four) sacred writings of ancient Brahmanism, hence 4. as symb. num.: 4. -- rig-ma (rig ma), वेदमातृ Veda-mother, Gāyatrī, a certain metre, verse and hymn of the Rigveda, personified as a deity Mil. -- rig-°dzin (rig 'dzin), from rig-pa °dzin-pa (rig pa 'dzin pa) to comprehend a science with ease, to be of quick parts Dzl., as partic.: a man of parts, a clever fellow; but usually rig-°dzin (rig 'dzin) (like rig-°ċaṅ (rig 'chang), of rarer occurrence), Ssk. विद्याधर, denotes a kind of spirits to whom a high degree of wisdom is attributed, like the Dākinīs. -- rig-śés (rig shes) the faculty of reason Tar. 90, 2, Schf.
rig-rig (rig rig), mig rig-rig byéd-pa (mig rig rig byed pa) or dúg-pa (dug pa) to look about, esp. in an anxious manner, shyly Tar., Mil.

1. family, lineage, extraction, birth, descent, rigs-rús (rigs rus) lineage and family Glr., mai rigs-su nyé-ba (ma'i rigs su nye ba) or gtógs-pa (gtogs pa) a relation by the mother's side Dzl.; emph.: noble birth or extraction: rigs-kyi bu (rigs kyi bu) or bú-mo (bu mo) noble or honoured sir! honoured madam! a respectful address, which is also more generally applied; thus in Thgr. it is the regular way of addressing the soul of a deceased person; mí-rigs (mi rigs) 1. the human race, mankind Cs. 2. nation, tribe Glr.; sdé-rigs (sde rigs) tribe Cs. 3. rarely = sex, mó-rigs (mo rigs) female sex Wdn. -- 2. in a special sense: caste, class in society, rank. In Tibet five ranks are usually distinguished. viz.: rgyál-rigs (rgyal rigs) royal state, royalty, brám-ze-rigs (bram ze rigs) caste of priests (Brahman caste), rjé-rigs (rje rigs) nobility, aristocracy, dmáṅs-rigs (dmangs rigs) the citizens, gdól-pai rigs (gdol pa'i rigs) the common people. When speaking of India, the appellations of these classes are applied to the castes of Brahmanism, although they do not correspond to each other in every respect. -- 3. kind, sort, species, groi rigs gsum yod (gro'i rigs gsum yod) there are three sorts of wheat, skád- (skad)
